title: aristotelia serrata
intro: Aristotelia serrata, also known as New Zealand tea tree or karamu, is a small shrub native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its serrated leaves and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been utilized to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Aristotelia serrata include its ability to combat infections, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, offering potential health benefits such as improved immune function and enhanced skin health. In herbal preparations, Aristotelia serrata is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
